differential_diagnoses: Gram-negative bacterial sepsis (from potential spontaneous bacterial peritonitis or translocation)
next_steps: Perform dengue virus testing (NS1 antigen, IgM/IgG serology, and RT-PCR)
Order a peripheral blood smear for malaria parasites (thick and thin films) and repeat malaria antigen testing
Initiate testing for Leptospira (PCR and MAT)
Obtain specialized infectious disease consultation and contact public health authorities
Monitor hemodynamics with point-of-care echocardiography to guide vasopressor and fluid titration
Perform diagnostic paracentesis to rule out spontaneous bacterial peritonitis
Consider early empirical treatment with doxycycline or penicillin G for leptospirosis pending diagnostic results
